summary: Verified SMTP encryption fix end-to-end. Backend: 8/8 pytest tests pass. Frontend: all new Email Settings fields render with data-testid, encryption dropdown auto-sets port (SSL=465, TLS=587), Send Test Email surfaces 400 auth-rejected messages from the mail server, Account page has smtp-encryption dropdown. Public booking still works with no SMTP configured (no crash).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is large (~1648 lines) — consider splitting into modules (auth, settings, bookings, email) for maintainability.
_smtp_send returns descriptive errors and correctly branches on ssl/tls/none — good fix.
Password masking on GET /api/settings works; sending '********' back preserves stored password (as documented).
seed_data_creation: None persisted. Settings reset to empty (business_email='', smtp_host='', smtp_user='', smtp_password='', smtp_encryption='tls', smtp_port=587). No test bookings with example.com emails remained.
context_for_next_testing_agent: SMTP encryption fix fully verified. Root-cause proof confirmed: SSL(465) and TLS(587) paths against smtp.gmail.com with wrong credentials return HTTP 400 with auth-rejected message (proves encrypted connection succeeded). Invalid host returns HTTP 400 with descriptive connect error. No HTTP 500s observed. Per-admin Account page has smtp-encryption dropdown at /api/auth/my-email-settings/test.
rca of the issue: Original bug: _smtp_send always called STARTTLS, so port-465 SSL hosts failed the handshake and reported a generic 'invalid' error. Fix (verified): _smtp_send now branches on smtp_encryption (ssl uses smtplib.SMTP_SSL directly on 465, tls uses SMTP+starttls on 587, none skips encryption) with auto-detect when port=465. Returns (ok, error) so real SMTP errors surface as HTTP 400. Confirmed via wrong-creds tests: gmail SSL/465 and TLS/587 both reach the auth stage and return specific 'username or password' error.
